passes.description: 308 Congress Street, Boston, Massachusetts, 02210 Map $12 admission per person ("Half Price Library Ticket") for up to 4 visitors. Children under 1 year old get in for free.

THIS IS A RETURNABLE PASS Coupon allows for free parking for one vehicle for the day at Hale during normal business hours and access to hiking Hale’s many trails. 25 Evans Way, Boston, Massachusetts, 02115 Map This pass allows for booking up to four Adult tickets at $5 each. Children under 18 always get in free and should be booked as child tickets. This library pass does not guarantee a reservation to the Museum.  If you have any issues reserving your tickets, you can call the Gardner Museum's Box Office at 617-278-5156.

1 Central Wharf, Boston, Massachusetts, 02110 Map This coupon allows free admission to the New England Aquarium for up to 4 people. Library coupons may not be used in conjunction with school or group visits, nor be applied toward Aquarium programs, Simons Theatre tickets, or combination tickets.

Located just steps away from downtown Boston, a visit to the Boch Center Wang Theatre will make you feel like you've been magically transported to a European Palace. Tours of the theatre are offered several days per week and share the rich history of the Wang Theatre over the past 100 years. Beyond the basic theatre history, a tour includes the opportunity to experience the Folk Americana Roots Hall of Fame (FARHOF). FARHOF showcases the history of American music through multiple exhibits that change quarterly. Visits are available at set times each day and full tours may not be available on all days. Please visit  www.bochcenter.org/tours  to learn more about the schedule and availability.

617-547-8300 The American Repertory Theater (A.R.T.) at Harvard University is a leading force in the American theater, producing groundbreaking work that is driven by risk-taking and passionate inquiry. A.R.T. was founded in 1980 by Robert Brustein, who served as Artistic Director until 2002, when he was succeeded by Robert Woodruff. Diane Paulus began her tenure as Terrie and Bradley Bloom Artistic Director in 2008.
This pass allows up to four patrons per visit. Each visitor will be charged $2.00. Children under two are admitted free.

Coupon code good for one boat rental per order. The American City Coalition (TACC) is a 501(c)(3) organization based in Roxbury’s Nubian Square. We tackle community-identified barriers through economic and workforce development, research, and policy strategies that expand pathways to meaningful opportunities in communities of color. Boston is a coastal city with 47 miles of shoreline; Boston Harbor is the city's largest public open space. Through our Waterways: Connecting Residents of Roxbury and Dorchester to Boston's Waterfront program, TACC is implementing strategies that reduce community-identified barriers to accessing the waterfront and increase the number of Roxbury and Dorchester residents benefitting from living in a coastal city. Reach out to us at  waterways@tamcc.org  or 857.308.3012 to learn more and get involved.
Free Greenway Carousel family admission (up to 4 individuals) for  one ride  ( e-coupon).

Redeem pass at either Harvard Museum of Natural History or the Peabody Museum. Note: This pass is limited to one booking per month per person.

Pass will admit up to 2 adults & 2 children at no charge. This is a disposable pass. Please note that your email reservation confirmation is NOT a substitute for the physical pass. You must have a physical pass to gain entry. Printed email confirmation vouchers will not be honored at any museum location. This pass is available to book at the Central and Jamaica Plain library branches. Please pick up your physical pass at the branch where you booked it. Note: This pass is limited to one booking per month per person.
Coupon admits 6 people at $9 per adult and $6 per child. Note: This pass is limited to one booking per month per person.

The Sandwich Glass Museum is dedicated to the history of the town in which it is located, with a special emphasis on the glass that made it famous in the 19th century. Thirteen galleries display their collection of old and modern examples of glass objects, including roughly 6,000 pieces produced at the Boston & Sandwich Glass Company in the 1800s on the site where the museum sits today. In addition, the museum hosts live demonstrations of glass-blowing. There is a gift store on the premises, which includes work by local glass-blowers.

Pass allows free admission for up to 4 people. This includes access to the Museum and Summer Lawn Events. Please consult website for events and hours. THIS IS A RETURNABLE PASS: This pass  must be returned the following day  of your scheduled reservation to the location where the pass was reserved. Passes reserved on Saturday can be returned on the following Monday. Note: This pass is limited to one booking per month per person.

passes.title_raw: How Do You See the World + Mapparium™ Globe
passes.museum_name: How Do You See the World + Mapparium™ Globe
passes.description: 210 Massachusetts Avenue, Boston, Massachusetts, 02115 Map With this pass, we welcome up to four adults free of charge to the Mapparium™ globe in the How Do You See the World? experience (value $6 per Adult). Youth 17 and younger are always free. All other exhibits in the How Do You See the World? experience are always free, as are weekend church tours, and neither require a ticket. You may redeem this pass for tickets onsite in the Welcome Hall; ticket reservations are not available ahead of time. All Mapparium showtimes are first come, first serve; this pass doesn't guarantee tickets for a specific time or day. Just print out this pass and bring it with you or display it on your phone or device.
